units.title: Production-only import checks reduce false positives
units.context: Architecture contract tests scanning modules that also contain `#[cfg(test)]` helper imports
units.insight: Import-boundary checks should target production sections to avoid test-only imports triggering invalid architectural failures.
units.suggested_action: Split source at `#[cfg(test)]` and enforce forbidden-edge patterns only on production content for adapter boundary tests.

units.title: Keep CLI contract updates end-to-end
units.context: When changing creation command flags and required inputs
units.insight: Command tree flags, runtime mappers, and user-facing suggestion strings drift unless updated in the same slice.
units.suggested_action: Pair every CLI contract edit with parser rejection tests for removed flags and updates to generated command hints.

units.title: Keep CLI parser, runtime mapping, and template tokens aligned
units.context: Adding new `adr new` flags required updates across clap action enums, command tree wiring, runtime ArgMatches extraction, and template rendering inputs.
units.insight: Parser and persistence changes stay reliable when command tests cover both parse-time option capture and file-level frontmatter serialization in one change set.
units.suggested_action: For every new CLI flag, add tests for command parsing and persisted artifact output before changing runtime behavior.

units.title: Evidence UX Needs Structured Inventory Layers
units.context: Rendering acceptance evidence directly in `story show`
units.insight: A clear split between linked proofs, supplementary artifacts, and media playback hints makes acceptance decisions possible without opening files manually.
units.suggested_action: Keep evidence rendering model-driven and test each layer (metadata, excerpts, missing warnings, placeholders) independently.

units.title: Centralize technique status before rendering
units.context: Multiple commands (`config show`, `verify recommend`) need the same detected/disabled/active evaluation.
units.insight: A shared status report API in the read model removes duplicated filtering logic and keeps recommendation output consistent across surfaces.
units.suggested_action: Route all verification-technique render paths through `resolve_technique_status_report` rather than command-local detection code.

units.title: Source-Scanning Contracts Catch Layer Drift Early
units.context: Enforcing adapter and read-model boundaries in a mixed legacy/refactor codebase where imports can regress silently.
units.insight: Lightweight file-content contract tests are effective guardrails for forbidden dependency edges and can be validated in normal unit-test flow.
units.suggested_action: Add/adjust architecture contract tests whenever a boundary or layering rule is introduced so regressions fail fast in CI.
units.applied: Added enforceable boundary tests and wired them into test compilation via main test module declarations.

units.title: Keep token inventories and CLI `new` surfaces coupled by drift tests
units.context: Token ownership policy spans templates and command interfaces; either side can drift silently without explicit coupling tests.
units.insight: A two-layer contract works best: template bucket tests catch unknown/out-of-bucket tokens while drift tests lock exact `new` command argument sets for ownership boundaries.
units.suggested_action: When adding new tokenized fields, update bucket inventories and expected `new` arg sets in the same change to keep policy deterministic.

units.title: Enforce policy invariants in application services, not command handlers
units.context: Story and voyage lifecycle commands need consistent enforcement of manual-verification acceptance and requirements-coverage gating.
units.insight: Putting enforcement in application services centralizes lifecycle invariants and avoids drift across multiple command entrypoints.
units.suggested_action: Keep command handlers thin and add service-level tests for every lifecycle policy; use architecture contract tests to block direct transition orchestration from handlers.

units.title: Deterministic Projection Requires Ordered Containers End-To-End
units.context: Rendering `next --parallel` output in both human and JSON projections
units.insight: Stable candidate sorting is not enough; projection containers must also preserve ordering or serialized output can still drift across runs.
units.suggested_action: Use ordered maps (`BTreeMap`) for projection payloads and shared projection helpers for all render paths.
units.applies_to: `src/cli/commands/management/next.rs` and other CLI JSON projection builders

units.title: Reuse the structural placeholder detector in runtime gates
units.context: Story submit/accept transitions need coherence checks aligned with doctor enforcement.
units.insight: Reusing `first_unfilled_placeholder_pattern` keeps runtime and doctor behavior consistent while avoiding duplicate marker logic.
units.suggested_action: Add lifecycle gate checks by composing existing structural validators before adding new regex or scanners.

units.title: Planning Show Parsing Needs Scaffold Filters
units.context: Extracting PRD summaries from partially authored templates
units.insight: Requirement parsing must explicitly ignore scaffold rows like `TODO`/template defaults or placeholder mode appears complete when it is not.
units.suggested_action: Keep placeholder filters and add fixture tests that assert empty summaries on scaffold-only PRDs.

units.title: Assert check identity and severity for hard-cutover gates
units.context: Updating terminal artifact coherence enforcement for doctor and story transitions
units.insight: Message-only assertions can pass even if a hard error silently downgrades to a warning; check-id plus severity assertions prevent this regression class
units.suggested_action: For each enforcement rule, add at least one integration test that asserts both `check_id` and `severity`

units.title: Keep Operational Metrics In A Single Read Model
units.context: Consolidating repeated queue/flow/status metrics used across diagnostics and next-decision logic
units.insight: A canonical projection DTO that embeds both flow metrics and status metrics removes drift and lets adapters format output without recalculating business metrics
units.suggested_action: Add read-model projection services first, then migrate every consumer to the projection API before deleting local metric structs

units.title: Shared template rendering reduces cross-command coupling
units.context: Multiple creation paths (story, epic, voyage, bearing, ADR, transitions) performing placeholder substitution
units.insight: Keeping placeholder substitution in command-local helpers increases coupling and makes cross-command refactors noisier than necessary.
units.suggested_action: Route all template substitution through `infrastructure::template_rendering::render` and enforce usage with architecture contract tests.

units.title: Thin Command Adapters Preserve Behavior During Refactors
units.context: Extracting orchestration out of CLI command handlers while keeping existing workflow behavior stable
units.insight: Moving orchestration to an application service is low-risk when command handlers become thin pass-through adapters and existing command tests remain the compatibility suite.
units.suggested_action: For future migrations, extract service logic first, then convert command files to wrappers and keep legacy helper behavior behind `#[cfg(test)]` shims only where needed.

units.title: Relocated Source Files May Break Compile-Time Template Paths
units.context: Moving `templates.rs` under `src/infrastructure` changed relative path depth for `include_str!` macro calls.
units.insight: Compile-time embedded asset paths are location-sensitive; module moves must include immediate path rebasing for `include_str!` constants.
units.suggested_action: After moving infrastructure modules, run a targeted compile/test immediately and patch relative asset paths before broader refactors.

units.title: Regression Parity Needs Cross-Command Coverage
units.context: During migration of command handlers to shared application/read-model layers
units.insight: Policy thresholds can drift silently unless `next` and `flow` are asserted together at the same boundary conditions
units.suggested_action: Add paired regression tests that validate both command-level decisions and dashboard summaries for each queue policy boundary
units.applied: Added `command_regression_tests` cases for human-block and flow-block boundaries plus lifecycle start/submit/accept chain

units.title: Queue-policy facades prevent decision/rendering drift
units.context: Queue classifications were being consumed directly by multiple modules (`next`, `flow/bottleneck`, and `state_machine/flow`) with repeated policy calls.
units.insight: A small read-model facade (`read_model::queue_policy`) creates one consumption surface for policy outputs while keeping source-of-truth thresholds in `policy::queue`.
units.suggested_action: Add architecture contracts for policy-facade usage whenever policy semantics are consumed by multiple adapters or decision paths.

units.title: Multi-Requirement Stories Can Create Queue Cycles
units.context: Stories in the same voyage referenced overlapping SRS IDs, and queue dependency derivation blocked all stories from becoming ready.
units.insight: Implementation dependency derivation is SRS-order based; a story that references both early and later requirements can create circular dependencies across siblings.
units.suggested_action: Keep each implementation story mapped to a primary SRS requirement in sequence, and reserve aggregate contract cleanup requirements for the final story.

units.title: Advisory parser keeps keel.toml resilient
units.context: Technique overrides need richer schema while core config loading should not fail when optional override blocks are malformed.
units.insight: Parsing overrides from raw TOML with per-field diagnostics allows invalid entries to be ignored safely without blocking normal command behavior.
units.suggested_action: Keep optional/advanced config surfaces advisory by default, then merge validated entries into canonical models with explicit diagnostics.

units.title: Work Item Comparator Is Not Lexical
units.context: Building deterministic pairwise vectors for story IDs with numeric suffixes
units.insight: `compare_work_item_ids` can order IDs differently from naive lexical sorting (for example `S10` before `S2`)
units.suggested_action: Use `compare_work_item_ids` for all deterministic work-item ordering and avoid hard-coded lexical expectations in tests

units.title: Build Typed Command Actions Before Dispatching
units.context: Refactoring large CLI dispatch trees while preserving behavior and clap argument contracts
units.insight: Converting `ArgMatches` into typed action enums at the boundary and routing through module `run(action)` functions keeps `main` focused on parsing while pushing interface adaptation into command-group modules
units.suggested_action: Keep adding action enums and single entrypoint adapters per command group so architecture tests can enforce delegation contracts cleanly

units.title: Enforce Root Layout With Contracts
units.context: Large module migrations where old root files can remain importable after moves
units.insight: Physical moves alone are not stable; contract tests must also assert forbidden `main.rs` module declarations and removed root file paths
units.suggested_action: Pair every structural move with architecture contracts that check both declaration edges and on-disk paths
units.applied: Added normalized-root and legacy-path assertions for all migrated root modules

units.title: Parse legacy forms but block execution paths
units.context: CLI hard cutovers where old invocations should fail fast with recovery guidance
units.insight: Keeping hidden legacy root args allows deterministic migration errors without relying on generic clap parse failures, while still forcing execution through the new subcommand path.
units.suggested_action: For future command cutovers, preserve temporary parse compatibility only for guidance and route all execution through explicit new subcommands.

units.title: Voyage Requirement Views Need Both AC And Verify Mapping
units.context: Building requirement-level voyage progress from story artifacts
units.insight: Requirement linkage should combine AC references and verify requirement IDs; relying on one source undercounts coverage/verification state.
units.suggested_action: Build requirement matrices from both marker channels, then deterministically sort rows and linked stories.

units.title: Catalog Entries Should Be Declarative And Sorted By ID
units.context: Defining a technique bank that will be extended by config/autodetection stories
units.insight: A stable schema plus ID-sorted built-ins gives deterministic output and a predictable merge base for later override/ranking stages.
units.suggested_action: Keep all built-ins in one constructor and enforce sort-by-ID before returning catalog vectors.

units.title: Frontmatter-rewrite adapters preserve markdown parity with low migration risk
units.context: Implementing filesystem repository/document adapters over existing `.keel` markdown files without changing domain/application behavior.
units.insight: Parsing existing frontmatter, serializing updated typed frontmatter, and reattaching the original body provides a practical parity-preserving persistence strategy while introducing port-based boundaries.
units.suggested_action: Reuse this adapter pattern for future repository migrations and add command-level integration points incrementally to avoid broad behavior shifts.

units.title: Report Pattern And Severity From One Shared Placeholder Extractor
units.context: Multiple doctor checks were independently detecting TODO/tokens and emitting inconsistent warning messages.
units.insight: A shared unresolved-pattern extractor enables deterministic detection and allows every check to emit the same actionable `pattern: ...` output while enforcing error severity.
units.suggested_action: Route all new scaffold/default-text checks through the shared extractor and assert severity/message structure in unit tests.

units.title: Centralized show projections reduce drift
units.context: Multiple show commands were independently parsing PRD/SRS/story evidence with diverging placeholder and ordering rules.
units.insight: A shared read-model projection layer stabilizes data contracts, keeps ordering deterministic, and lets renderers remain thin.
units.suggested_action: Add new planning/read surfaces by extending `read_model::planning_show` first, then adapt renderer output only.

units.title: Unknown Context Should Force Risk Floor
units.context: Pairwise scoring for partial architectural metadata in `next --parallel`
units.insight: Unresolved semantic context is easiest to keep safe when scoring applies an explicit risk floor and confidence ceiling instead of only additive penalties
units.suggested_action: Keep conservative fallback thresholds as first-class scoring invariants and assert them directly in tests

units.title: Intake Stage Must Be Canonical In Creation Code
units.context: Story creation had stage branching by voyage state
units.insight: Canonical defaults are safest when enforced once in creation code and not left to template/state-condition combinations.
units.suggested_action: Keep `story new` stage assignment unconditional and test it across unscoped/draft/planned scopes.

units.title: Stage-gate scaffold checks to avoid noisy early warnings
units.context: Adding scaffold/default text diagnostics to doctor checks
units.insight: Stage filtering is critical: terminal-only checks avoid penalizing in-progress drafting while still hard-failing review-complete states.
units.suggested_action: Reuse a shared unresolved-pattern detector and explicitly gate by story stage (`needs-human-verification`, `done`) for terminal coherency rules.

units.title: Keep Lifecycle Command Handlers As Thin Adapters
units.context: Refactoring voyage and epic lifecycle commands to align with application-service orchestration boundaries
units.insight: Moving orchestration into a dedicated application service lets command modules stay stable adapters while preserving behavior through existing command tests
units.suggested_action: Add use-case methods first, then delegate command `run` entrypoints to those methods and update cross-command callsites to service APIs

units.title: Interface Adapters Should Delegate Instead Of Recompute
units.context: When both flow rendering and diagnostics commands need the same projection outputs
units.insight: Duplicated adapter-level projection/load/render paths drift quickly and should be collapsed behind a single interface that consumes canonical read-model DTOs
units.suggested_action: Keep one shared capacity interface and enforce delegation from command modules through architecture contracts
units.applied: Delegated diagnostics capacity command to `flow::capacity` and added explicit contract test for shared interface usage

units.title: Centralized recommendation projection keeps show commands coherent
units.context: Multiple read commands need consistent recommendation output while using different local data sources.
units.insight: A shared recommendation report model plus per-command input extraction avoids drift between epic/voyage/story rendering.
units.suggested_action: Add new recommendation behavior in `verification_techniques` first, then wire each show command through the same renderer helper.

units.title: Keep recommendation sourcing decoupled from planning read surfaces
units.context: Planning read commands and verification-technique discovery can drift when both surfaces try to rank/recommend techniques.
units.insight: Moving recommendation concerns to dedicated commands (`config show` inventory + `verify recommend`) keeps planning show outputs focused on planning state and avoids mixed concerns.
units.suggested_action: Keep epic/voyage/story show projections limited to planning progress/evidence summaries; centralize recommendation logic in verification/config read models.

units.title: Keep Blocker Schema Shared Across Human and JSON Paths
units.context: Rendering pairwise confidence blockers in CLI and machine-readable output
units.insight: A single blocker model (`story_id`, `blocked_by_story_id`, `reasons`, `confidence`) makes it easy to keep human and JSON outputs in sync
units.suggested_action: Build future blocker explanations from the same canonical blocker payload and only vary presentation

units.title: Keep Token Names Equal To Frontmatter Keys
units.context: Template scaffolds and their renderer replacement maps drift when placeholder names are generic (`date`, `datetime`) instead of schema names.
units.insight: Canonical token names that mirror frontmatter fields (`created_at`, `updated_at`) remove ambiguity and make drift detection/test assertions straightforward.
units.suggested_action: For any new template token, require a matching model/frontmatter key name (or explicit documented exception) and add a regression guard against legacy aliases.

units.title: Event-First Cross-Aggregate Orchestration Preserves Boundaries
units.context: Story start/accept and voyage completion previously embedded cross-aggregate calls inside lifecycle services.
units.insight: Emitting explicit domain events and routing follow-on actions through a process manager keeps use cases focused while preserving existing behavior.
units.suggested_action: Keep cross-aggregate progression in process managers and add event/action tests whenever new lifecycle automation is introduced.
units.applied: Introduced DomainEvent + DomainProcessManager and rewired lifecycle services to emit events.

units.title: Path-Wide Module Moves Need Import Rewrite First
units.context: Relocating top-level module families (`commands`, `flow`, `next`) to a new root (`cli`) while preserving behavior.
units.insight: Bulk file moves are low-risk only when import rewrites and architecture path fixtures are updated in the same slice; otherwise compile passes but contract tests drift.
units.suggested_action: For physical normalization stories, perform move + import rewrite + fixture path updates atomically before running full test and doctor checks.

units.title: Context Maps Need Enforceable Ownership
units.context: Defining DDD boundaries for migration planning before code extraction starts
units.insight: A useful context map must include both owned module paths and forbidden coupling rules, not just conceptual labels.
units.suggested_action: For each new context, document ownership, allowed seams, and forbidden dependencies in one table before implementation stories begin.

units.title: Declarative Frontmatter Patches Reduce Drift Across Commands
units.context: Multiple commands had bespoke line-replacement logic for status/scope/timestamp edits, increasing drift risk and maintenance overhead.
units.insight: A shared mutation service with `set/remove` operations preserves behavior while eliminating duplicated frontmatter edit loops.
units.suggested_action: Route future frontmatter changes through shared mutation primitives and add service-level tests for insertion/replacement/removal semantics.

units.title: Ports Should Mirror Aggregate Boundaries
units.context: Defining persistence abstractions before filesystem adapters are extracted from command modules
units.insight: Repository ports are easier to evolve when contracts are grouped by aggregate boundary (story, voyage, epic, bearing, adr) plus one board snapshot port for orchestration use cases.
units.suggested_action: Keep port interfaces in the application layer and defer adapter wiring to subsequent stories to minimize behavior risk during migration.

units.title: Deterministic ranking requires total-order tie breaks
units.context: Recommendation scores can tie across techniques when confidence and keyword matches are equivalent.
units.insight: Deterministic ordering is guaranteed only when ranking sorts by score and then by stable id as a total-order tie breaker.
units.suggested_action: Keep recommendation outputs sorted by `(score desc, id asc)` and normalize lists/sets before scoring.

units.title: Prefer direct status flags over aggregated recommendation blocks
units.context: Config introspection commands where automation depends on deterministic machine-readable state
units.insight: A per-technique flag matrix (`detected`, `disabled`, `active`) is a better contract boundary than mixed narrative sections because it cleanly separates inventory from recommendation logic.
units.suggested_action: Keep config/read commands focused on canonical state and move advisory ranking/commentary to dedicated recommend commands.

units.title: Greedy Threshold Gate Gives Deterministic Safe Subset
units.context: Selecting parallel-ready stories from pairwise confidence scores
units.insight: Sorting candidates by canonical work-item comparator before threshold filtering yields deterministic, reproducible safe subsets
units.suggested_action: Keep canonical ID ordering and missing-pair fallback confidence (`0.0`) as hard invariants in gate logic

units.title: Matrix Contracts Need Both Narrative and Table Forms
units.context: Defining a dependency contract that engineers can read quickly and tests can consume with minimal interpretation
units.insight: A single layer table is not enough for review; adding a compact `From \\ To` matrix reduces ambiguity about allowed and forbidden dependencies.
units.suggested_action: Keep layer contracts in two forms: descriptive per-layer rules plus a normalized matrix that can be translated directly into architecture tests.

units.title: Rollout Docs Need Explicit Gate Ownership
units.context: Finalizing migration voyages where multiple maintainers coordinate acceptance and release sequencing
units.insight: Checklist quality improves when each gate and rollout step is phrased as an explicit maintainer action with clear command references
units.suggested_action: Keep voyage-local migration checklists with completion criteria, gate commands, rollout order, and deferred-item tracking
units.applied: Added `MIGRATION_CHECKLIST.md` for `1vwq9wpT7` and linked it from voyage documents

units.title: Frontmatter Field Additions Need Builder + Literal Sweep
units.context: Adding a new key to `StoryFrontmatter` that is constructed in many tests and read models
units.insight: `#[serde(default)]` handles runtime parsing, but compile-time struct literals and test builders still require explicit wiring or defaults to avoid breakage and hidden drift in fixture generation.
units.suggested_action: When adding frontmatter fields, immediately update `TestStory`, `StoryFactory`, and all explicit `StoryFrontmatter { ... }` literals in one slice before running broader checks.

units.title: Coherence Checks Need Canonical Pair Normalization
units.context: Detecting reciprocal `blocked_by` contradictions in doctor checks
units.insight: Pair-level diagnostics become deterministic and deduplicated only when pair IDs are normalized (`min/max`) before reporting.
units.suggested_action: Always canonicalize relationship IDs before emitting pair-based doctor findings.

units.title: Canonical read models remove adapter drift
units.context: When both flow rendering and diagnostics need the same capacity semantics
units.insight: Duplicated DTOs and charge enums across adapters force conversion shims and create drift risk in UI logic.
units.suggested_action: Keep one projection type in `read_model` and make interface modules thin adapters over that projection.

units.title: Catalog Entries Should Be Declarative And Sorted By ID
units.context: Defining a technique bank that will be extended by config/autodetection stories
units.insight: A stable schema plus ID-sorted built-ins gives deterministic output and a predictable merge base for later override/ranking stages.
units.suggested_action: Keep all built-ins in one constructor and enforce sort-by-ID before returning catalog vectors.

units.title: Centralized recommendation projection keeps show commands coherent
units.context: Multiple read commands need consistent recommendation output while using different local data sources.
units.insight: A shared recommendation report model plus per-command input extraction avoids drift between epic/voyage/story rendering.
units.suggested_action: Add new recommendation behavior in `verification_techniques` first, then wire each show command through the same renderer helper.

units.context: Technique overrides need richer schema while core config loading should not fail when optional override blocks are malformed.
units.insight: Parsing overrides from raw TOML with per-field diagnostics allows invalid entries to be ignored safely without blocking normal command behavior.
units.suggested_action: Keep optional/advanced config surfaces advisory by default, then merge validated entries into canonical models with explicit diagnostics.

units.context: Recommendation scores can tie across techniques when confidence and keyword matches are equivalent.
units.insight: Deterministic ordering is guaranteed only when ranking sorts by score and then by stable id as a total-order tie breaker.
units.suggested_action: Keep recommendation outputs sorted by `(score desc, id asc)` and normalize lists/sets before scoring.

units.title: Centralize technique status before rendering
units.context: Multiple commands (`config show`, `verify recommend`) need the same detected/disabled/active evaluation.
units.insight: A shared status report API in the read model removes duplicated filtering logic and keeps recommendation output consistent across surfaces.
units.suggested_action: Route all verification-technique render paths through `resolve_technique_status_report` rather than command-local detection code.

units.title: Keep recommendation sourcing decoupled from planning read surfaces
units.context: Planning read commands and verification-technique discovery can drift when both surfaces try to rank/recommend techniques.
units.insight: Moving recommendation concerns to dedicated commands (`config show` inventory + `verify recommend`) keeps planning show outputs focused on planning state and avoids mixed concerns.
units.suggested_action: Keep epic/voyage/story show projections limited to planning progress/evidence summaries; centralize recommendation logic in verification/config read models.

units.context: CLI hard cutovers where old invocations should fail fast with recovery guidance
units.insight: Keeping hidden legacy root args allows deterministic migration errors without relying on generic clap parse failures, while still forcing execution through the new subcommand path.
units.suggested_action: For future command cutovers, preserve temporary parse compatibility only for guidance and route all execution through explicit new subcommands.

units.suggested_action: Keep config/read commands focused on canonical state and move advisory ranking/commentary to dedicated recommend commands.

units.insight: Canonical defaults are safest when enforced once in creation code and not left to template/state-condition combinations.
units.suggested_action: Keep `story new` stage assignment unconditional and test it across unscoped/draft/planned scopes.

units.suggested_action: Keep one shared capacity interface and enforce delegation from command modules through architecture contracts
units.applied: Delegated diagnostics capacity command to `flow::capacity` and added explicit contract test for shared interface usage

units.insight: Physical moves alone are not stable; contract tests must also assert forbidden `main.rs` module declarations and removed root file paths
units.suggested_action: Pair every structural move with architecture contracts that check both declaration edges and on-disk paths
units.applied: Added normalized-root and legacy-path assertions for all migrated root modules

units.title: Declarative Frontmatter Patches Reduce Drift Across Commands

units.insight: A shared mutation service with `set/remove` operations preserves behavior while eliminating duplicated frontmatter edit loops.
units.suggested_action: Route future frontmatter changes through shared mutation primitives and add service-level tests for insertion/replacement/removal semantics.

units.title: Ports Should Mirror Aggregate Boundaries
units.context: Defining persistence abstractions before filesystem adapters are extracted from command modules
units.insight: Repository ports are easier to evolve when contracts are grouped by aggregate boundary (story, voyage, epic, bearing, adr) plus one board snapshot port for orchestration use cases.
units.suggested_action: Keep port interfaces in the application layer and defer adapter wiring to subsequent stories to minimize behavior risk during migration.

units.title: Production-only import checks reduce false positives
units.context: Architecture contract tests scanning modules that also contain `#[cfg(test)]` helper imports
units.insight: Import-boundary checks should target production sections to avoid test-only imports triggering invalid architectural failures.
units.suggested_action: Split source at `#[cfg(test)]` and enforce forbidden-edge patterns only on production content for adapter boundary tests.

units.title: Regression Parity Needs Cross-Command Coverage
units.context: During migration of command handlers to shared application/read-model layers
units.insight: Policy thresholds can drift silently unless `next` and `flow` are asserted together at the same boundary conditions
units.suggested_action: Add paired regression tests that validate both command-level decisions and dashboard summaries for each queue policy boundary
units.applied: Added `command_regression_tests` cases for human-block and flow-block boundaries plus lifecycle start/submit/accept chain

units.title: Rollout Docs Need Explicit Gate Ownership
units.context: Finalizing migration voyages where multiple maintainers coordinate acceptance and release sequencing
units.insight: Checklist quality improves when each gate and rollout step is phrased as an explicit maintainer action with clear command references
units.suggested_action: Keep voyage-local migration checklists with completion criteria, gate commands, rollout order, and deferred-item tracking
units.applied: Added `MIGRATION_CHECKLIST.md` for `1vwq9wpT7` and linked it from voyage documents

units.title: Build Typed Command Actions Before Dispatching
units.context: Refactoring large CLI dispatch trees while preserving behavior and clap argument contracts
units.insight: Converting `ArgMatches` into typed action enums at the boundary and routing through module `run(action)` functions keeps `main` focused on parsing while pushing interface adaptation into command-group modules
units.suggested_action: Keep adding action enums and single entrypoint adapters per command group so architecture tests can enforce delegation contracts cleanly

units.title: Source-Scanning Contracts Catch Layer Drift Early
units.context: Enforcing adapter and read-model boundaries in a mixed legacy/refactor codebase where imports can regress silently.
units.insight: Lightweight file-content contract tests are effective guardrails for forbidden dependency edges and can be validated in normal unit-test flow.
units.suggested_action: Add/adjust architecture contract tests whenever a boundary or layering rule is introduced so regressions fail fast in CI.
units.applied: Added enforceable boundary tests and wired them into test compilation via main test module declarations.

units.title: Matrix Contracts Need Both Narrative and Table Forms
units.context: Defining a dependency contract that engineers can read quickly and tests can consume with minimal interpretation
units.insight: A single layer table is not enough for review; adding a compact `From \\ To` matrix reduces ambiguity about allowed and forbidden dependencies.
units.suggested_action: Keep layer contracts in two forms: descriptive per-layer rules plus a normalized matrix that can be translated directly into architecture tests.

units.title: Context Maps Need Enforceable Ownership
units.context: Defining DDD boundaries for migration planning before code extraction starts
units.insight: A useful context map must include both owned module paths and forbidden coupling rules, not just conceptual labels.
units.suggested_action: For each new context, document ownership, allowed seams, and forbidden dependencies in one table before implementation stories begin.

units.title: Event-First Cross-Aggregate Orchestration Preserves Boundaries
units.context: Story start/accept and voyage completion previously embedded cross-aggregate calls inside lifecycle services.
units.insight: Emitting explicit domain events and routing follow-on actions through a process manager keeps use cases focused while preserving existing behavior.
units.suggested_action: Keep cross-aggregate progression in process managers and add event/action tests whenever new lifecycle automation is introduced.
units.applied: Introduced DomainEvent + DomainProcessManager and rewired lifecycle services to emit events.

units.title: Enforce policy invariants in application services, not command handlers
units.context: Story and voyage lifecycle commands need consistent enforcement of manual-verification acceptance and requirements-coverage gating.
units.insight: Putting enforcement in application services centralizes lifecycle invariants and avoids drift across multiple command entrypoints.
units.suggested_action: Keep command handlers thin and add service-level tests for every lifecycle policy; use architecture contract tests to block direct transition orchestration from handlers.

units.title: Keep Lifecycle Command Handlers As Thin Adapters
units.context: Refactoring voyage and epic lifecycle commands to align with application-service orchestration boundaries
units.insight: Moving orchestration into a dedicated application service lets command modules stay stable adapters while preserving behavior through existing command tests
units.suggested_action: Add use-case methods first, then delegate command `run` entrypoints to those methods and update cross-command callsites to service APIs

units.title: Thin Command Adapters Preserve Behavior During Refactors
units.context: Extracting orchestration out of CLI command handlers while keeping existing workflow behavior stable
units.insight: Moving orchestration to an application service is low-risk when command handlers become thin pass-through adapters and existing command tests remain the compatibility suite.
units.suggested_action: For future migrations, extract service logic first, then convert command files to wrappers and keep legacy helper behavior behind `#[cfg(test)]` shims only where needed.

units.title: Assert check identity and severity for hard-cutover gates
units.context: Updating terminal artifact coherence enforcement for doctor and story transitions
units.insight: Message-only assertions can pass even if a hard error silently downgrades to a warning; check-id plus severity assertions prevent this regression class
units.suggested_action: For each enforcement rule, add at least one integration test that asserts both `check_id` and `severity`

units.title: Report Pattern And Severity From One Shared Placeholder Extractor
units.context: Multiple doctor checks were independently detecting TODO/tokens and emitting inconsistent warning messages.
units.insight: A shared unresolved-pattern extractor enables deterministic detection and allows every check to emit the same actionable `pattern: ...` output while enforcing error severity.
units.suggested_action: Route all new scaffold/default-text checks through the shared extractor and assert severity/message structure in unit tests.

units.title: Reuse the structural placeholder detector in runtime gates
units.context: Story submit/accept transitions need coherence checks aligned with doctor enforcement.
units.insight: Reusing `first_unfilled_placeholder_pattern` keeps runtime and doctor behavior consistent while avoiding duplicate marker logic.
units.suggested_action: Add lifecycle gate checks by composing existing structural validators before adding new regex or scanners.

units.title: Stage-gate scaffold checks to avoid noisy early warnings
units.context: Adding scaffold/default text diagnostics to doctor checks
units.insight: Stage filtering is critical: terminal-only checks avoid penalizing in-progress drafting while still hard-failing review-complete states.
units.suggested_action: Reuse a shared unresolved-pattern detector and explicitly gate by story stage (`needs-human-verification`, `done`) for terminal coherency rules.

units.title: Keep Token Names Equal To Frontmatter Keys
units.context: Template scaffolds and their renderer replacement maps drift when placeholder names are generic (`date`, `datetime`) instead of schema names.
units.insight: Canonical token names that mirror frontmatter fields (`created_at`, `updated_at`) remove ambiguity and make drift detection/test assertions straightforward.
units.suggested_action: For any new template token, require a matching model/frontmatter key name (or explicit documented exception) and add a regression guard against legacy aliases.

units.title: Keep CLI parser, runtime mapping, and template tokens aligned
units.context: Adding new `adr new` flags required updates across clap action enums, command tree wiring, runtime ArgMatches extraction, and template rendering inputs.
units.insight: Parser and persistence changes stay reliable when command tests cover both parse-time option capture and file-level frontmatter serialization in one change set.
units.suggested_action: For every new CLI flag, add tests for command parsing and persisted artifact output before changing runtime behavior.

units.title: Keep CLI contract updates end-to-end
units.context: When changing creation command flags and required inputs
units.insight: Command tree flags, runtime mappers, and user-facing suggestion strings drift unless updated in the same slice.
units.suggested_action: Pair every CLI contract edit with parser rejection tests for removed flags and updates to generated command hints.

units.title: Keep token inventories and CLI `new` surfaces coupled by drift tests
units.context: Token ownership policy spans templates and command interfaces; either side can drift silently without explicit coupling tests.
units.insight: A two-layer contract works best: template bucket tests catch unknown/out-of-bucket tokens while drift tests lock exact `new` command argument sets for ownership boundaries.
units.suggested_action: When adding new tokenized fields, update bucket inventories and expected `new` arg sets in the same change to keep policy deterministic.

units.title: Greedy Threshold Gate Gives Deterministic Safe Subset
units.context: Selecting parallel-ready stories from pairwise confidence scores
units.insight: Sorting candidates by canonical work-item comparator before threshold filtering yields deterministic, reproducible safe subsets
units.suggested_action: Keep canonical ID ordering and missing-pair fallback confidence (`0.0`) as hard invariants in gate logic

units.title: Frontmatter Field Additions Need Builder + Literal Sweep
units.context: Adding a new key to `StoryFrontmatter` that is constructed in many tests and read models
units.insight: `#[serde(default)]` handles runtime parsing, but compile-time struct literals and test builders still require explicit wiring or defaults to avoid breakage and hidden drift in fixture generation.
units.suggested_action: When adding frontmatter fields, immediately update `TestStory`, `StoryFactory`, and all explicit `StoryFrontmatter { ... }` literals in one slice before running broader checks.

units.title: Deterministic Projection Requires Ordered Containers End-To-End
units.context: Rendering `next --parallel` output in both human and JSON projections
units.insight: Stable candidate sorting is not enough; projection containers must also preserve ordering or serialized output can still drift across runs.
units.suggested_action: Use ordered maps (`BTreeMap`) for projection payloads and shared projection helpers for all render paths.
units.applies_to: `src/cli/commands/management/next.rs` and other CLI JSON projection builders

units.title: Coherence Checks Need Canonical Pair Normalization
units.context: Detecting reciprocal `blocked_by` contradictions in doctor checks
units.insight: Pair-level diagnostics become deterministic and deduplicated only when pair IDs are normalized (`min/max`) before reporting.
units.suggested_action: Always canonicalize relationship IDs before emitting pair-based doctor findings.

units.title: Keep Blocker Schema Shared Across Human and JSON Paths
units.context: Rendering pairwise confidence blockers in CLI and machine-readable output
units.insight: A single blocker model (`story_id`, `blocked_by_story_id`, `reasons`, `confidence`) makes it easy to keep human and JSON outputs in sync
units.suggested_action: Build future blocker explanations from the same canonical blocker payload and only vary presentation

units.title: Unknown Context Should Force Risk Floor
units.context: Pairwise scoring for partial architectural metadata in `next --parallel`
units.insight: Unresolved semantic context is easiest to keep safe when scoring applies an explicit risk floor and confidence ceiling instead of only additive penalties
units.suggested_action: Keep conservative fallback thresholds as first-class scoring invariants and assert them directly in tests

units.title: Work Item Comparator Is Not Lexical
units.context: Building deterministic pairwise vectors for story IDs with numeric suffixes
units.insight: `compare_work_item_ids` can order IDs differently from naive lexical sorting (for example `S10` before `S2`)
units.suggested_action: Use `compare_work_item_ids` for all deterministic work-item ordering and avoid hard-coded lexical expectations in tests
